testcase.classname: API - Notes REST API Validation
testcase.name: TS-API-01 - Login API returns valid JWT auth token
testcase.system-out: When the user attempts API login with valid credentials.....................passed Then the API login response status should be 200............................passed And the API response should contain a valid auth token......................passed And the API response time should be within 4 seconds........................passed

testcase.classname: API - Notes REST API Validation
testcase.name: TS-API-03 - GET notes returns list for authenticated user
testcase.system-out: Given I authenticate via API and store the token............................passed When I call GET /notes with the auth token..................................passed Then the GET /notes response should be 200..................................passed And the response should contain a list of notes.............................passed And the API response time should be within 4 seconds........................passed

testcase.classname: API - Notes REST API Validation
testcase.name: TS-API-02 - Create note via API and verify it appears in GET notes
testcase.system-out: Given I authenticate via API and store the token............................passed When I create a note via API with category "Work", title "API Created Note", and description "Created directly via RestAssured API call".passed Then the API response status should be 200..................................passed And the API response time should be within 4 seconds........................passed When I call GET /notes with the auth token..................................passed Then the API response should contain the created note.......................passed When I delete the previously created API note...............................passed

testcase.classname: API - Notes REST API Validation
testcase.name: TS-API-05 - Delete a note via API and verify removal
testcase.system-out: Given I authenticate via API and store the token............................passed When I create a note via API with category "Personal", title "Note For Deletion Test", and description "This note will be deleted via API".passed Then the API response status should be 200..................................passed When I delete the previously created API note...............................passed And the API response time should be within 4 seconds........................passed

testcase.classname: API - Notes REST API Validation
testcase.name: TS-API-06 - All API endpoints respond within 4 seconds

failure.message: Performance failures: POST /users/login: 2189ms | (threshold: 2000ms) expected [true] but found [false]

testcase.failure: java.lang.AssertionError: Performance failures: POST /users/login: 2189ms | (threshold: 2000ms) expected [true] but found [false] at org.testng.Assert.fail(Assert.java:111) at org.testng.Assert.failNotEquals(Assert.java:1578) at org.testng.Assert.assertTrue(Assert.java:57) at com.capstone.stepdefs.ApiStepDefs.allTestedApiEndpointsShouldRespondWithin2Seconds(ApiStepDefs.java:465) at ✽.all tested API endpoints should respond within 4 seconds(file:///C:/Users/RAUNAK/Downloads/Sprint%203%20d/capstone_project/src/test/resources/features/api/api_tests.feature:70)
testcase.system-out: Given I authenticate via API and store the token............................passed Then all tested API endpoints should respond within 4 seconds...............failed

testcase.classname: API - Notes REST API Validation
testcase.name: TS-API-06 - All API endpoints respond within 4 seconds
testcase.system-out: Given I authenticate via API and store the token............................passed Then all tested API endpoints should respond within 4 seconds...............passed

testcase.classname: API - Notes REST API Validation
testcase.name: TS-API-03b - GET notes with different auth scenarios - Example #1.1
testcase.system-out: Given I authenticate via API and store the token............................passed When I call GET /notes with the auth token..................................passed Then the GET /notes response should be 200..................................passed

testcase.classname: API - Notes REST API Validation
testcase.name: TS-API-03b - GET notes with different auth scenarios - Example #1.2
testcase.system-out: Given I authenticate via API and store the token............................passed When I call GET /notes with the auth token..................................passed Then the GET /notes response should be 200..................................passed

testcase.classname: E2E - Hybrid UI and API Cross-Layer Validation
testcase.name: TS-E2E-01 - Note created via UI appears in the API response
testcase.system-out: Given I authenticate via API and store the token............................passed And the user is logged in to the notes dashboard............................passed When the user creates a note with category "Home", title "E2E Sync Test Note", and description "Created in browser - must appear in API".passed Then the created note should appear in the notes list.......................passed And the note created in UI should appear in the API response................passed And the API response time should be within 2 seconds........................passed And the note category and title should match between UI and API.............passed

testcase.classname: E2E - Hybrid UI and API Cross-Layer Validation
testcase.name: TS-E2E-02 - Note deleted via API disappears from the UI
testcase.system-out: Given I authenticate via API and store the token............................passed When I create a note via API with category "Work", title "E2E Reverse Sync Note", and description "Created via API - will be deleted via API".passed Then the API response status should be 200..................................passed And the user is logged in to the notes dashboard............................passed And the note with title "E2E Reverse Sync Note" should appear in the notes list.passed When I delete the note via API using the stored note ID.....................passed And the note deleted via API should not appear in the UI....................passed

testcase.classname: E2E - Hybrid UI and API Cross-Layer Validation
testcase.name: TS-E2E-04 - Complete note lifecycle: create in UI, verify in API, delete via API, verify removal in UI

failure.message: API response time 2488ms exceeds 2000ms threshold expected [true] but found [false]

testcase.failure: java.lang.AssertionError: API response time 2488ms exceeds 2000ms threshold expected [true] but found [false] at org.testng.Assert.fail(Assert.java:111) at org.testng.Assert.failNotEquals(Assert.java:1578) at org.testng.Assert.assertTrue(Assert.java:57) at com.capstone.stepdefs.ApiStepDefs.theApiResponseTimeShouldBeWithin(ApiStepDefs.java:284) at ✽.the API response time should be within 2 seconds(file:///C:/Users/RAUNAK/Downloads/Sprint%203%20d/capstone_project/src/test/resources/features/e2e/hybrid_e2e.feature:85)
testcase.system-out: Given I authenticate via API and store the token............................passed And the user is logged in to the notes dashboard............................passed When the user creates a note with category "Personal", title "Full Lifecycle E2E Note", and description "Complete lifecycle: UI create, API verify, API delete, UI confirm".passed Then the created note should appear in the notes list.......................passed And the note created in UI should appear in the API response................passed And the note category and title should match between UI and API.............passed When I find the note ID in the API and store it.............................passed And I delete the note via API using the stored note ID......................passed Then the API response status should be 200..................................passed And the note deleted via API should not appear in the UI....................passed And the API response time should be within 2 seconds........................failed

testcase.classname: E2E - Hybrid UI and API Cross-Layer Validation
testcase.name: TS-E2E-04 - Complete note lifecycle: create in UI, verify in API, delete via API, verify removal in UI
testcase.system-out: Given I authenticate via API and store the token............................passed And the user is logged in to the notes dashboard............................passed When the user creates a note with category "Personal", title "Full Lifecycle E2E Note", and description "Complete lifecycle: UI create, API verify, API delete, UI confirm".passed Then the created note should appear in the notes list.......................passed And the note created in UI should appear in the API response................passed And the note category and title should match between UI and API.............passed When I find the note ID in the API and store it.............................passed And I delete the note via API using the stored note ID......................passed Then the API response status should be 200..................................passed And the note deleted via API should not appear in the UI....................passed And the API response time should be within 2 seconds........................passed

testcase.classname: Negative - Security and Error Handling Validation
testcase.name: TS-NEG-01 - Access rejected when no auth token is provided
testcase.system-out: When I call GET /notes without an auth token................................passed Then the response should not be authorised..................................passed And the response should contain an error message............................passed And the API response time should be within 5 seconds........................passed

testcase.classname: Negative - Security and Error Handling Validation
testcase.name: TS-NEG-02 - Access rejected with a gibberish auth token
testcase.system-out: When I call GET /notes with an invalid token "this-is-not-a-valid-token-xyz123".passed Then the response should not be authorised..................................passed And the response should contain an error message............................passed

testcase.classname: Negative - Security and Error Handling Validation
testcase.name: TS-NEG-02b - Access rejected with an expired-format JWT token
testcase.system-out: When I call GET /notes with an invalid token "eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.INVALID.SIGNATURE".passed Then the response should not be authorised..................................passed

testcase.classname: Negative - Security and Error Handling Validation
testcase.name: TS-NEG-03 - UI login rejected for invalid credentials - Example #1.1
testcase.system-out: Given the user is on the login page.........................................passed When the user logs in with email "nonexistent@capstone.com" and password "ValidPass123".passed Then the login error message should be displayed............................passed

testcase.classname: Negative - Security and Error Handling Validation
testcase.name: TS-NEG-03 - UI login rejected for invalid credentials - Example #1.2
testcase.system-out: Given the user is on the login page.........................................passed When the user logs in with email "raunak@gmail.com" and password "incorrectpass".passed Then the login error message should be displayed............................passed

testcase.classname: Negative - Security and Error Handling Validation
testcase.name: TS-NEG-03 - UI login rejected for invalid credentials - Example #1.3
testcase.system-out: Given the user is on the login page.........................................passed When the user logs in with email "hacker@evil.com" and password "password"..passed Then the login error message should be displayed............................passed

testcase.classname: Negative - Security and Error Handling Validation
testcase.name: TS-NEG-04 - API login endpoints must reject bad payloads - Example #1.1
testcase.system-out: When the user attempts API login with email "wrong@example.com" and password "WrongPass123".passed Then the API login response status should be 401............................passed And the response should contain an error message............................passed

testcase.classname: Negative - Security and Error Handling Validation
testcase.name: TS-NEG-04 - API login endpoints must reject bad payloads - Example #1.2
testcase.system-out: When the user attempts API login with email "raunak@gmail.com" and password "BadPassword".passed Then the API login response status should be 401............................passed And the response should contain an error message............................passed

testcase.classname: UI - Login and Authentication
testcase.name: TS-UI-01 - Successful login with valid credentials
testcase.system-out: Given the user is on the login page.........................................passed When the user logs in with valid credentials................................passed Then the user should be redirected to the notes dashboard...................passed And the page load time should be acceptable.................................passed

testcase.classname: UI - Login and Authentication
testcase.name: TS-UI-02d - Login with various invalid credentials - Example #1.1
testcase.system-out: Given the user is on the login page.........................................passed When the user logs in with email "notregistered@test.com" and password "Password123".passed Then the login error message should be displayed............................passed

testcase.classname: UI - Notes CRUD Operations
testcase.name: TS-UI-03 - Create a note and verify it appears in the list
testcase.system-out: Given the user is logged in to the notes dashboard..........................passed When the user creates a note with category "Home", title "My First Test Note", and description "This note was created by Selenium automation".passed Then the note with title "My First Test Note" should appear in the notes list.passed And the dashboard page load time should be within limits....................passed

testcase.classname: UI - Notes CRUD Operations
testcase.name: TS-UI-07 - Delete a note and verify it is removed from the list
testcase.system-out: Given the user is logged in to the notes dashboard..........................passed When the user creates a note with category "Work", title "Note To Be Deleted", and description "This note will be deleted".passed And the note with title "Note To Be Deleted" should appear in the notes list.passed When the user deletes the note titled "Note To Be Deleted"..................passed Then the note with title "Note To Be Deleted" should NOT appear in the notes list.passed
